name: kube_scheduler
explanation: kube-scheduler exposes Prometheus metrics on /metrics, but the port and scheme have changed between Kubernetes versions: legacy clusters expose unauthenticated http on :10251, while modern (1.13+) clusters expose authenticated https on :10259 and require a service-account bearer token. The shipped auto_conf.yaml encodes this with possible_prometheus_urls listing four URL/port/scheme combinations the check tries in order. Authentication is automated via the in-cluster service-account token mounted at the standard path with bearer_token_auth=tls_only, so the Agent running in-cluster needs no user-supplied credential. Discovery requires the probe to walk the four candidates and respect TLS/auth quirks.
notes: Pattern G (multiple plausible URL paths) with a twist: tokens are read from a known in-cluster path so 'credentials' are not user-provided. The integration already implements the discovery via possible_prometheus_urls and ad_identifiers=[kube-scheduler].
